mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the last month lowers discount rates and improves equity valuation multiples, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in most periods following a 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE increase of at least 0.3 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns more often than not, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO over the prior month indicates stronger industrial output and better earnings momentum, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after monthly INDPRO increases, or if the signal's hit rate falls below 52% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise over the same month, household income and retail demand are improving together, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone in walk-forward tests, or if triggered cases fail to exceed 55% positive 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A HOUST decline of more than 5% over two months signals weaker construction demand and softer cyclical activity, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not negative on average after the trigger, or if the signal loses significance after revision adjustment across a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration, measured by CPIAUCSL rising faster than its prior 3-month average while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, creates a policy lag that compresses real returns and hurts equities over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds its prior 3-month average while FEDFUNDS < 1%
regime_hint: inflation surprise with delayed policy response
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-rate condition does not precede negative 20-day returns, or if SPX is positive in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies labor-market deterioration that reduces expected corporate revenue and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration signal fails to outperform each component alone, or if the 5-day hit rate is below 50% over any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary regime, weak GDPC1 growth combined with falling RSAFS confirms synchronized demand contraction, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined demand contraction signal does not remain negative in recessionary windows, or if it fails to produce lower returns than non-recession baselines over a 60-day horizon.
